channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Friday that Denmark has "underinvested" in Greenland’s security and demanded that Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump pushes to take over the Danish territory. The pointed remarks came as Vance visited U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP CROWTHER, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Amanda Knox's advice for American linked to Punta Cana missing persons case
item.description: Amanda Knox was a student studying abroad in Italy when she was accused of murdering friend Meredith Kercher. She spent nearly four years in an Italian prison before she was exonerated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New wave of smaller, cheaper nuclear reactors sends US states racing to attract the industry
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — With the promise of newer, cheaper nuclear power on the horizon, U.S. states are vying to position themselves to build and supply the industry's next generation as policymakers consider expanding subsidies and paving over regulatory obstacles.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Kidnapped 6-year-old girl discovered during traffic stop: troopers
item.description: A trooper's discovery during a routine traffic stop last month led to the rescue of a 6-year-old girl who had been kidnapped for weeks, according to the Kansas Highway Patrol.
item.dc:creator: Barry Owens, KTLA 5

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump administration cannot immediately deport Tufts student
item.description: A federal judge recently ruled a Tufts University grad student cannot be immediately deported. Meanwhile, ICE agents made their latest student detainment near the University of Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbia University's interim president steps down and returns to former post
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University’s interim president Katrina Armstrong has resigned, returning to her post running the New York school’s medical center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Drenching rains along the Texas-Mexico border trapped hundreds of people in flooded homes and in cars stranded in high waters, scrambling rescue crews to calls for help that continued Friday even as the downpours let up. At least four people died, including some who drowned.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oregon city at heart of high court homelessness ruling blocked from enforcing camping ban
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — An Oregon judge issued a preliminary injunction Friday blocking the city at the heart of a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on homeless encampments from enforcing its camping rules unless it meets certain conditions, as part of a lawsuit filed by advocates.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Carolina wildfire keeps growing as firefighters protect homes
item.description: A large wildfire in the South Carolina mountains has doubled in size on each of the last three days. But fire crews have been able to keep the blaze away from structures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Stolen truck pursuit ends with violent, multi-vehicle pileup in Southern California
item.description: One of the more dangerous and destructive police pursuits in recent memory unfolded on live television in south Los Angeles County Friday afternoon, involving a driver in a white stolen pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk says xAI buys social media platform X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday his artificial intelligence firm xAI has bought his social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck vandals caught on video scrawling 'Nazis' and swastika: NYPD
item.description: The New York Police Department Hate Crime Task Force is investigating after two men were caught on camera carving "Nazis" and a swastika onto a Cybertruck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah bans fluoride in public drinking water, a first in the US
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah has become the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water, pushing past opposition from dentists and national health organizations who warn the move will lead to medical problems that disproportionately affect low-income communities. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration investigates Maine for claims of withholding gender transitions from parents
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The administration of President Donald Trump is investigating Maine with a claim that dozens of school districts in the state violate federal law by withholding information about students' gender transitioning from parents. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wall Street tumbles on worries about slower economy, inflation
item.description: Another wipeout is swamping Wall Street Friday on worries about potential worsening inflation and the U.S. economy slowing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jury from outside of Memphis will hear the case of 3 former officers charged in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A jury from outside of Memphis will be seated in the state court trial of three former police officers charged with second-degree murder in the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023, a judge ruled Friday. Shelby County Judge James Jones Jr.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The top-rated seafood restaurant in every state, according to Yelp
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: David Childs, architect of One World Trade Center that rose on Twin Towers site after 9/11, dies
item.description: David Childs, the lead architect of the One World Trade Center skyscraper that rose from the site where the Twin Towers collapsed in New York City during the 9/11 attacks, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Baby's corpse, busted casket found on grounds of West Virginia cemetery
item.description: A baby's corpse and a busted casket were found at a cemetery in Salt Rock, West Virginia, on Thursday evening, according to the Cabell County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barren, Fox 5

item.title: This is the top-rated seafood restaurant in California, according to Yelp
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner. Knowing this, the analysts at Yelp have sought to identify the top-rated seafood restaurants in each state.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Snake covered by Endangered Species Act killed at Michigan park
item.description: Officials are urging “understanding” over “fear” after a federally protected snake was recently found killed at the Matthaei Botanical Gardens and Nichols Arboretum in Ann Arbor.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk to travel to Wisconsin ahead of judicial race, hand out $1M checks
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk announced late Thursday he would be traveling to Wisconsin ahead of a high-stakes state Supreme Court race to talk with voters and hand out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ investigating California universities over affirmative action
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) announced Thursday it will be investigating multiple California universities to ensure they are in compliance with a Supreme Court ruling banning affirmative action in admissions.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Americans’ outlook on economy becomes a bit more pessimistic: Poll
item.description: Around 57 percent of those earning under $50,000 said their financial situation is “bad,” while 39 percent argued it is “good,” the poll found.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia mom’s Walmart trip devolves into ‘tug-of-warring’ in desperate attempt to save her son
item.description: A Georgia mother is in shock and a man is behind bars after he allegedly tried to grab a 2-year-old child while the family was shopping in a local Walmart.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration moves to end union rights for many federal workers
item.description: The order targets agencies it says have a national security mission but many of the departments don’t have a strict national security connection.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump order targets 'divisive narratives' in Smithsonian museums
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday signed an executive order seeking to exert more control over what is displayed at Smithsonian museums and federal sites, citing what the White House called “divisive narratives” promoted by certain museums.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Stanford, Cal and UCLA investigated in Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: The Trump administration has opened investigations into the admissions policies at Stanford University and three campuses within the University of California system, including UC Berkeley, UCLA and UC Irvine, the Department of Justice said Thursday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Daughter of slain Hamas hostages files suit against Columbia groups
item.description: The lawsuit claims Khalil and other student groups at Columbia helped spread Hamas messaging on Columbia University's campus.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Another federal judge blocks Trump policy banning transgender troops in the military
item.description: TACOMA, Wash. (AP) — A U.S. judge in Washington state has blocked enforcement of President Donald Trump’s order banning transgender people from serving in the military, the second nationwide injunction against the policy in as many weeks. The order Thursday from U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: First rain and then fire chase people from their homes in North and South Carolina
item.description: When Nicole Taylor and her family moved to their new home in the South Carolina mountains six months ago, the gorgeous view of Table Rock Mountain was the clincher.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge says heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional as states face mounting lawsuits
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A federal judge's rebuke of Texas housing prisoners in lockups without air conditioning stopped short of ordering a fix before summer in what has become of the country's biggest lawsuits over keeping prisoners safe during dangerous extreme heat. But U.S.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Yolanda Saldívar, killer of Selena, denied parole
item.description: The woman convicted of killing Tejano music legend Selena Quintanilla-Perez has been denied parole after spending decades behind bars for fatally shooting the young singer at a Texas motel in 1995, the state’s parole board announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rubio says at least 300 foreign students' visas have been revoked
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Thursday at least 300 foreign students have had their visas revoked amid the Trump administration's immigration agenda, a far higher number than was previously known.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: As many as 6 hurricanes forecast to strike US this season
item.description: “It only takes one landfall to create a devastating season,” AccuWeather chief meteorologist Jonathan Porter said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Retired immigration judge recalls unaccompanied infants in need of lawyers
item.description: A retired federal immigration judge on Tuesday spoke out against the Trump administration for canceling federal funds for lawyers to represent unaccompanied minors who illegally cross the border into the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5
